clinical_consequence: Severe bradycardia, symptomatic hypotension, increased risk of ventricular arrhythmia
timing_notes: Reduce carvedilol dose and monitor heart rate and blood pressure; ECG surveillance recommended

effect: Verapamil inhibits P-gp efflux of digoxin, increasing digoxin serum concentrations by 50-75%
clinical_consequence: Digoxin toxicity with bradycardia, heart block, nausea, and arrhythmias
timing_notes: Reduce digoxin dose by 30-50% at initiation of verapamil; monitor digoxin levels and ECG

effect: Furosemide-induced hypokalemia and hypomagnesemia increase myocardial sensitivity to digoxin toxicity
clinical_consequence: Enhanced digoxin toxicity risk including arrhythmias even at therapeutic digoxin levels
timing_notes: Monitor electrolytes (K+, Mg2+) and digoxin levels; supplement potassium/magnesium as needed

effect: Thiazide-induced sodium depletion leads to compensatory increase in proximal tubular lithium reabsorption, raising lithium levels by up to 40%
clinical_consequence: Lithium toxicity: tremor, confusion, ataxia, seizures, renal failure
timing_notes: Check lithium levels within 4-5 days of starting or changing thiazide dose; reduce lithium dose proactively

effect: Amiodarone inhibits CYP3A4 and CYP2C8, dramatically increasing simvastatin AUC and risk of myotoxicity
clinical_consequence: Rhabdomyolysis, severe myopathy, acute kidney injury
timing_notes: Limit simvastatin to 20 mg/day with amiodarone; consider alternative statin (pravastatin, rosuvastatin)

effect: Cyclosporine inhibits OATP1B1, CYP3A4, and P-gp, increasing atorvastatin AUC up to 8-fold
clinical_consequence: High risk of rhabdomyolysis and acute kidney injury
timing_notes: Use pravastatin (low dose) or fluvastatin as safer alternatives; avoid atorvastatin with cyclosporine

effect: Ketoconazole (strong dual CYP3A4 and P-gp inhibitor) increases apixaban AUC by approximately 2-fold
clinical_consequence: Significantly increased bleeding risk including major hemorrhage
timing_notes: Avoid combination; if antifungal needed, use fluconazole with caution and monitor closely

effect: Rifampin induces CYP3A4 and P-gp, reducing rivaroxaban AUC by approximately 50%
clinical_consequence: Loss of anticoagulant efficacy; increased risk of thromboembolic events
timing_notes: Avoid concurrent use; consider parenteral anticoagulation during rifampin therapy

effect: Verapamil inhibits P-gp, increasing dabigatran AUC by approximately 50-75%
clinical_consequence: Increased risk of bleeding, particularly gastrointestinal and intracranial hemorrhage
timing_notes: Reduce dabigatran dose to 110 mg BID if creatinine clearance 30-50 mL/min; take dabigatran 2 hours before verapamil

effect: Amiodarone inhibits P-gp, increasing edoxaban exposure by approximately 40%
clinical_consequence: Increased bleeding risk
timing_notes: Consider reducing edoxaban dose to 30 mg daily; monitor for bleeding signs

effect: MAO inhibition combined with TCA serotonergic effects precipitates serotonin syndrome and hypertensive crisis
clinical_consequence: Potentially fatal serotonin syndrome: hyperthermia, seizures, cardiovascular collapse
timing_notes: Allow 14-day washout after stopping MAOI before starting TCA; 14 days after TCA before MAOI

effect: Additive serotonergic activity and CYP2D6 competition; tramadol is a weak serotonin reuptake inhibitor
clinical_consequence: Serotonin syndrome risk; tramadol-induced seizure risk increased with SNRI co-use
timing_notes: Use alternative analgesics; if co-use unavoidable, start tramadol at lowest dose with close monitoring

effect: Bupropion is a potent CYP2D6 inhibitor, reducing conversion of tamoxifen to active endoxifen by 60-75%
clinical_consequence: Significantly reduced tamoxifen efficacy in hormone-receptor positive breast cancer treatment
timing_notes: Use alternative antidepressants (sertraline, citalopram, escitalopram) that have minimal CYP2D6 inhibition

effect: Additive CNS depression; mirtazapine has antihistaminergic and alpha-adrenergic sedative properties
clinical_consequence: Excessive sedation, psychomotor impairment, respiratory depression in high doses
timing_notes: Both agents have sedating properties; avoid nighttime driving; monitor for respiratory depression

effect: Fluconazole inhibits CYP3A4, increasing trazodone plasma levels significantly
clinical_consequence: QT prolongation, excessive sedation, priapism, serotonin syndrome risk
timing_notes: Reduce trazodone dose and monitor QTc interval; use lowest effective dose

effect: Erythromycin inhibits CYP3A4, increasing quetiapine AUC by approximately 54%; both prolong QT
clinical_consequence: Additive QT prolongation risk; torsades de pointes; ventricular arrhythmia
timing_notes: Avoid combination; if unavoidable, obtain baseline ECG and monitor QTc closely

effect: Additive neurotoxicity; lithium may impair haloperidol clearance; both lower seizure threshold
clinical_consequence: Encephalopathy, neurotoxicity, irreversible brain damage; acute EPS worsening
timing_notes: Monitor for neurotoxicity signs; keep lithium in lower therapeutic range (0.6-0.8 mEq/L); watch for fever

effect: Additive CNS and respiratory depression; combined GABA-A potentiation and opioid receptor agonism
clinical_consequence: Profound respiratory depression, loss of consciousness, death; FDA black box warning
timing_notes: If co-prescription necessary, use lowest doses and shortest duration; advise patient on overdose risks

effect: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4, increasing eszopiclone AUC by approximately 2.2-fold
clinical_consequence: Excessive sedation, amnesia, respiratory depression, complex sleep behaviors
timing_notes: Reduce eszopiclone dose to 1 mg at bedtime during clarithromycin therapy

effect: MAOB inhibition by selegiline impairs amphetamine metabolism; synergistic catecholamine release
clinical_consequence: Hypertensive crisis, serotonin syndrome, hyperthermia, seizures, cardiovascular collapse
timing_notes: Allow 14 days after selegiline discontinuation before starting amphetamines

effect: Methylphenidate may inhibit CYP2C9, reducing warfarin metabolism and increasing INR
clinical_consequence: Enhanced anticoagulant effect; increased bleeding risk
timing_notes: Monitor INR more frequently when starting, stopping, or changing methylphenidate dose

effect: Fluoxetine potently inhibits CYP2D6, increasing atomoxetine AUC 6-8-fold; combined noradrenergic effects
clinical_consequence: Increased cardiovascular effects (tachycardia, hypertension), mood changes, potential serotonin syndrome
timing_notes: Start atomoxetine at reduced dose (0.5 mg/kg/day) and titrate slowly; monitor BP and heart rate

effect: Valproate inhibits UDP-glucuronosyltransferase (UGT), reducing lamotrigine clearance and nearly doubling lamotrigine levels
clinical_consequence: Lamotrigine toxicity: diplopia, ataxia, serious rash (Stevens-Johnson syndrome risk)
timing_notes: Use lamotrigine starting doses half of standard; titrate more slowly; monitor lamotrigine levels

effect: Synergistic CNS and respiratory depression; both agents inhibit voltage-gated calcium channels and modulate GABA
clinical_consequence: Severe respiratory depression, overdose, and death; risk increased significantly compared to opioid alone
timing_notes: FDA black box warning for CNS depressant combination; if co-prescribed, monitor respiratory status

effect: Metoclopramide is a dopamine antagonist that directly opposes levodopa's therapeutic effects in the CNS
clinical_consequence: Loss of Parkinson's disease control; worsening tremor, rigidity, akinesia
timing_notes: Use domperidone (peripheral dopamine antagonist) for nausea in Parkinson's patients instead

effect: Ciprofloxacin inhibits OCT2 (organic cation transporter 2), reducing renal tubular secretion of pramipexole by ~50%
clinical_consequence: Increased pramipexole plasma levels; dopaminergic side effects (nausea, hypotension, hallucinations, somnolence)
timing_notes: Reduce pramipexole dose or monitor closely during ciprofloxacin course

effect: Entacapone inhibits COMT; linezolid inhibits MAO; combined catecholamine accumulation
clinical_consequence: Hypertensive crisis, serotonin syndrome, severe cardiovascular events
timing_notes: Do not use together; allow adequate washout period; consider alternative antibiotics

effect: Penicillins compete with methotrexate for renal tubular secretion via OAT transporters, reducing methotrexate clearance
clinical_consequence: Methotrexate toxicity: myelosuppression, mucositis, hepatotoxicity, nephrotoxicity
timing_notes: Avoid penicillins during methotrexate therapy; use alternative antibiotics; monitor CBC and LFTs

effect: Azithromycin alters gut flora and may inhibit CYP3A4, increasing warfarin effect
clinical_consequence: Enhanced anticoagulant effect with increased bleeding risk; INR elevation
timing_notes: Monitor INR closely during and for 1 week after azithromycin course; adjust warfarin dose as needed

effect: Divalent and trivalent cations chelate doxycycline in the GI tract, forming insoluble complexes
clinical_consequence: Reduced doxycycline absorption by up to 90%; treatment failure
timing_notes: Take doxycycline 2-3 hours before or 4-6 hours after antacids, calcium supplements, or dairy

effect: Both are ototoxic agents; furosemide alters cochlear electrolyte transport enhancing aminoglycoside uptake
clinical_consequence: Synergistic ototoxicity (sensorineural hearing loss, vestibular damage); nephrotoxicity risk
timing_notes: Avoid concurrent IV furosemide with aminoglycosides; monitor audiometry, serum creatinine, and drug levels

effect: TMP blocks renal epithelial sodium channel (ENaC) like amiloride, reducing potassium excretion; combined hyperkalemia risk
clinical_consequence: Severe hyperkalemia; cardiac arrhythmias, sudden death
timing_notes: Monitor serum potassium within 3-7 days; use with caution in renal impairment

effect: Synergistic nephrotoxicity; piperacillin-tazobactam may increase vancomycin AUC through uncertain mechanisms
clinical_consequence: Acute kidney injury incidence significantly higher with combination vs. vancomycin alone
timing_notes: Monitor renal function daily; use vancomycin AUC-guided dosing; consider alternative beta-lactam

effect: Iron chelates ciprofloxacin in the GI tract, reducing absorption by up to 75%
clinical_consequence: Subtherapeutic ciprofloxacin levels; antibiotic treatment failure
timing_notes: Take ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after iron supplements

effect: Iron chelates levodopa and carbidopa in the GI tract, reducing their absorption
clinical_consequence: Reduced levodopa effectiveness; worsening Parkinson's symptoms
timing_notes: Administer iron at least 2 hours before or after levodopa/carbidopa
high_risk_groups: Parkinson's disease patients with iron deficiency anemia

effect: Iron forms insoluble chelate complexes with tetracycline, reducing tetracycline absorption by ~80%
clinical_consequence: Subtherapeutic antibiotic levels; treatment failure
timing_notes: Take tetracycline at least 2 hours before or 4 hours after iron
high_risk_groups: patients requiring tetracycline therapy for infections

effect: Iron reduces mycophenolic acid absorption through chelation and may alter enterohepatic recirculation
clinical_consequence: Reduced immunosuppressive efficacy; increased risk of transplant rejection
timing_notes: Administer iron at least 4 hours apart from mycophenolate; monitor drug levels

effect: Oral vancomycin can reduce the efficacy of probiotic bacteria strains that are sensitive to glycopeptides
clinical_consequence: Reduced probiotic colonization and efficacy; however, Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium often intrinsically resistant
timing_notes: Separate probiotic from oral vancomycin by at least 2 hours; assess strain-specific resistance

effect: Caffeine inhibits CYP1A2, competing with clozapine metabolism; clozapine levels may increase
clinical_consequence: Clozapine toxicity: sedation, hypersalivation, seizures; sudden caffeine reduction may lower clozapine levels
timing_notes: Advise stable caffeine consumption; monitor clozapine levels if caffeine intake changes significantly

effect: EGCG inhibits OATP1A2 transporter, reducing nadolol bioavailability by up to 85%
clinical_consequence: Significantly reduced antihypertensive and antianginal effect of nadolol
timing_notes: Take nadolol at least 4 hours before green tea or EGCG supplements

effect: DHEA converts peripherally to estrogen, potentially opposing tamoxifen's anti-estrogen mechanism
clinical_consequence: Potential reduction in tamoxifen efficacy; theoretical increased risk of hormone-receptor positive breast cancer recurrence
timing_notes: Advise patients with ER+ breast cancer to avoid DHEA supplements during tamoxifen therapy

effect: Kratom alkaloids (mitragynine) have opioid receptor agonist activity and CNS depressant properties
clinical_consequence: Profound CNS depression, respiratory depression, coma; additive with benzodiazepines
timing_notes: Advise against kratom use with any CNS depressant; no established safe dose

effect: CBD inhibits CYP2C19 and CYP3A4, significantly increasing levels of clobazam and its active metabolite N-desmethylclobazam
clinical_consequence: Enhanced sedation, ataxia, and somnolence; clobazam toxicity
timing_notes: Reduce clobazam dose when initiating CBD; monitor serum levels and adverse effects closely

effect: NAC potentiates nitric oxide release from nitroglycerin and may overcome nitroglycerin tolerance
clinical_consequence: Severe hypotension, headache, and cardiovascular instability; synergistic vasodilation
timing_notes: Use IV NAC with IV nitroglycerin only in monitored setting; reduce nitroglycerin dose

effect: L-arginine is nitric oxide precursor; PDE5 inhibitors prevent cGMP degradation; combined vasodilatory effect
clinical_consequence: Severe hypotension, dizziness, syncope, erectile dysfunction therapy risk
timing_notes: Avoid concurrent high-dose L-arginine with PDE5 inhibitors; particularly dangerous in cardiovascular disease

effect: Probiotics may alter gut microbiome affecting tacrolimus metabolism and systemic exposure
clinical_consequence: Unpredictable changes in tacrolimus levels; risk of organ rejection or tacrolimus toxicity (nephrotoxicity)
timing_notes: Monitor tacrolimus trough levels closely if probiotics are started or stopped; consult transplant team

effect: Elderberry contains immunostimulatory polyphenols that may counteract immunosuppressive therapy
clinical_consequence: Potential immune activation; increased risk of organ rejection in transplant recipients
timing_notes: Advise transplant patients to avoid elderberry and other immunostimulatory supplements

effect: High-dose biotin (>10 mg/day) interferes with biotin-streptavidin immunoassays used in many laboratory tests
clinical_consequence: Falsely low TSH and falsely high T3/T4 values mimicking hyperthyroidism or Graves' disease
timing_notes: Stop biotin supplementation for at least 48-72 hours before blood draws for thyroid tests; inform lab

effect: High-dose niacin (>1 g/day) combined with statins increases myopathy risk; additive muscle toxicity
clinical_consequence: Myopathy, rhabdomyolysis risk; also, niacin may cause hepatotoxicity additive with statin
timing_notes: Limit niacin dose to <1 g/day with statins; monitor CK, LFTs, and for muscle pain symptoms

effect: Chromium enhances insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism; may additively lower blood glucose with metformin
clinical_consequence: Hypoglycemia risk, particularly with higher chromium doses or additional hypoglycemic agents
timing_notes: Monitor blood glucose more frequently when initiating chromium; adjust medications if needed

effect: ALA's antioxidant properties may theoretically reduce cisplatin-induced oxidative stress that contributes to cancer cell killing
clinical_consequence: Potential reduction in cisplatin chemotherapy efficacy; may protect tumor cells
timing_notes: Avoid high-dose antioxidant supplements during active chemotherapy; discuss with oncologist

effect: Psyllium fiber can bind carbamazepine in the GI tract, reducing its absorption
clinical_consequence: Reduced carbamazepine efficacy; breakthrough seizures
timing_notes: Take carbamazepine at least 2 hours before psyllium and avoid taking together

effect: Activated charcoal adsorbs virtually all oral medications in the GI tract, preventing their absorption
clinical_consequence: Complete therapeutic failure of any concurrently taken oral medication
timing_notes: Administer regular medications at least 1-2 hours before or 2-4 hours after activated charcoal (non-emergency use)

effect: Silver ions may chelate quinolone antibiotics, reducing their absorption
clinical_consequence: Reduced antibiotic bioavailability; potential treatment failure
timing_notes: Avoid concurrent use; take antibiotics at least 2-4 hours before colloidal silver

effect: Dong quai contains phytoestrogens that have estrogenic and anti-estrogenic activity; may compete with or add to exogenous estrogen
clinical_consequence: Unpredictable changes in estrogenic effects; potential increased risk of endometrial stimulation or reduced HRT efficacy
timing_notes: Advise patients on HRT or tamoxifen to avoid dong quai; inform oncologist

effect: SAMe increases serotonin and dopamine synthesis; combined with SNRI may cause additive serotonergic excess
clinical_consequence: Serotonin syndrome: agitation, tremor, diarrhea, hyperthermia
timing_notes: Do not use SAMe concurrently with SNRIs, SSRIs, TCAs, or MAOIs

effect: Phosphatidylserine may have mild anticoagulant/antiplatelet properties, potentially additive with warfarin
clinical_consequence: Increased bleeding risk; INR elevation
timing_notes: Monitor INR when starting or stopping phosphatidylserine supplements; advise physician

effect: Resveratrol inhibits CYP3A4 and P-gp, potentially increasing apixaban exposure; also has intrinsic antiplatelet activity
clinical_consequence: Increased bleeding risk with additive anticoagulant and antiplatelet effects
timing_notes: Caution with high-dose resveratrol supplements (>500 mg/day) with DOACs; monitor for bleeding

effect: Quercetin inhibits CYP3A4, P-gp, and OATP1B1, significantly increasing cyclosporine bioavailability and AUC
clinical_consequence: Cyclosporine toxicity: nephrotoxicity, neurotoxicity, hypertension, hepatotoxicity
timing_notes: Avoid quercetin supplements in patients on cyclosporine; monitor drug levels if inadvertent exposure

effect: Carbamazepine is a potent CYP3A4 and UGT inducer, reducing ethinyl estradiol and progestin levels by 40-70%
clinical_consequence: Contraceptive failure and unintended pregnancy; carbamazepine may also be affected by estrogen
timing_notes: Use alternative non-hormonal contraception or higher-dose formulations; consider IUD

effect: Additive GI mucosal damage; corticosteroids thin gastric mucosal lining while NSAIDs inhibit prostaglandin-mediated cytoprotection
clinical_consequence: Significantly increased risk of peptic ulcer disease, GI bleeding, and perforation
timing_notes: Consider proton pump inhibitor prophylaxis; limit aspirin dose; monitor for GI symptoms

effect: Testosterone inhibits CYP2C9 metabolism of warfarin and may affect clotting factor synthesis
clinical_consequence: Significant INR elevation; major bleeding risk
timing_notes: Monitor INR closely when starting, stopping, or changing testosterone; adjust warfarin dose accordingly

effect: Beta-blockers mask sympathomimetic hypoglycemia warning symptoms (tachycardia, tremor); non-selective beta-blockers also impair glycogenolysis
clinical_consequence: Hypoglycemia may be prolonged and unrecognized; sweating (cholinergically mediated) is not masked
timing_notes: Use cardioselective beta-blockers (metoprolol, atenolol) preferentially; monitor blood glucose more frequently

effect: Cyclosporine inhibits CYP3A4, OATP1B1, and P-gp, causing up to 6-fold increase in simvastatin AUC
clinical_consequence: Severe myopathy and rhabdomyolysis
timing_notes: Simvastatin is contraindicated with cyclosporine; use fluvastatin (low dose), pravastatin, or rosuvastatin (low dose)

effect: Azithromycin may inhibit CYP3A4 and P-gp-mediated efflux of tacrolimus, increasing tacrolimus AUC
clinical_consequence: Tacrolimus toxicity: nephrotoxicity, neurotoxicity, QT prolongation
timing_notes: Monitor tacrolimus trough levels within 3-5 days of starting azithromycin; adjust dose as needed

effect: Antacids reduce gastric pH and chelate mycophenolate, reducing its absorption by approximately 40%
clinical_consequence: Subtherapeutic mycophenolate levels; risk of organ rejection
timing_notes: Administer mycophenolate at least 2 hours before or after antacids; consider PPIs as alternative

effect: Both agents prolong cardiac QT interval via hERG potassium channel blockade; additive effect
clinical_consequence: Torsades de pointes, ventricular fibrillation, sudden cardiac death
timing_notes: Avoid concurrent use; use alternative antiemetics (metoclopramide, dexamethasone, prochlorperazine)

effect: Aprepitant inhibits CYP3A4 acutely then induces it chronically; dexamethasone AUC increased by ~2-fold when given with aprepitant
clinical_consequence: Excessive corticosteroid effects; Cushingoid symptoms if standard dexamethasone dose not reduced
timing_notes: Reduce oral dexamethasone dose by approximately 50% when combined with aprepitant in CINV regimens

effect: PPIs reduce renal tubular secretion of methotrexate by inhibiting ABC transporters; may also compete for renal excretion
clinical_consequence: Elevated methotrexate levels; toxicity including myelosuppression, mucositis, nephrotoxicity
timing_notes: Monitor methotrexate levels and toxicity signs; consider temporary PPI discontinuation during high-dose MTX

effect: Sucralfate binds quinolones in the GI tract, reducing absorption by 30-80% through aluminum chelation
clinical_consequence: Subtherapeutic antibiotic levels; treatment failure
timing_notes: Administer fluoroquinolone at least 2 hours before or 6 hours after sucralfate

effect: Metoclopramide accelerates gastric emptying, increasing rate and extent of cyclosporine absorption
clinical_consequence: Higher cyclosporine peak concentrations; increased risk of nephrotoxicity and other toxicity
timing_notes: Monitor cyclosporine trough and peak levels when metoclopramide is used; adjust dose accordingly

effect: Synergistic CNS and respiratory depression; both modulate spinal pain pathways via complementary mechanisms
clinical_consequence: Significantly increased risk of fatal respiratory depression; FDA black box warning
timing_notes: Use lowest effective doses; educate patients on overdose risks; prescribe naloxone rescue

effect: Diltiazem inhibits CYP3A4, significantly increasing fentanyl plasma concentrations
clinical_consequence: Profound opioid toxicity: respiratory depression, sedation, hypotension
timing_notes: Reduce fentanyl dose when initiating diltiazem; monitor sedation and respiratory status

effect: Additive CNS and respiratory depression despite buprenorphine's partial agonist ceiling effect
clinical_consequence: Fatal overdose risk; FDA black box warning for opioid-benzodiazepine combinations
timing_notes: If combination unavoidable (e.g., OUD + anxiety), use minimum doses with close monitoring

effect: Celecoxib inhibits CYP2C9, reducing warfarin (S-enantiomer) metabolism; platelet inhibition worsens bleeding
clinical_consequence: Significantly elevated INR; major bleeding including GI hemorrhage
timing_notes: Monitor INR within 1 week of starting/stopping celecoxib; use gastroprotection; minimize NSAID dose and duration

effect: NSAIDs inhibit renal prostaglandin synthesis, reducing renal blood flow and lithium clearance, increasing lithium levels by 15-60%
clinical_consequence: Lithium toxicity: tremor, ataxia, confusion, seizures, renal failure
timing_notes: Avoid NSAIDs in patients on lithium; use acetaminophen for pain instead; if NSAID used, check lithium level in 4-5 days

effect: Cyclobenzaprine (TCA-related structure) and tramadol both have serotonergic and CNS depressant properties
clinical_consequence: Serotonin syndrome risk; excessive sedation; seizure threshold lowering
timing_notes: Use alternative muscle relaxants (methocarbamol, tizanidine) with tramadol if needed; monitor closely

effect: Amlodipine weakly inhibits CYP3A4, increasing simvastatin exposure; FDA limits simvastatin dose
clinical_consequence: Increased myopathy risk with simvastatin doses >20 mg/day when combined with amlodipine
timing_notes: Limit simvastatin to 20 mg/day with amlodipine per FDA guidance; consider switching to another statin

effect: Amlodipine inhibits CYP3A4, potentially increasing tacrolimus levels; both are metabolized by CYP3A4
clinical_consequence: Tacrolimus toxicity risk: nephrotoxicity, neurotoxicity, hyperkalemia
timing_notes: Monitor tacrolimus trough levels when amlodipine is initiated or dose changed

effect: Fluvoxamine potently inhibits CYP1A2 (and CYP2C9), reducing ramelteon clearance and increasing AUC by approximately 190-fold
clinical_consequence: Profound sedation, cognitive impairment; unpredictable sleep effects
timing_notes: Ramelteon is contraindicated with fluvoxamine; use alternative sleep agents

effect: Synergistic CNS and respiratory depression; pregabalin potentiates opioid-induced respiratory depression
clinical_consequence: Significantly increased risk of respiratory failure and death; FDA black box warning
timing_notes: Prescribe at lowest effective doses; assess respiratory function; provide naloxone rescue

effect: Additive CNS depression; carbamazepine induces CYP3A4 and may modestly reduce levetiracetam levels
clinical_consequence: Increased sedation, dizziness, diplopia; modest reduction in levetiracetam efficacy possible
timing_notes: Monitor for excessive CNS side effects; seizure diary; EEG monitoring if needed

effect: Oxcarbazepine induces CYP3A4, reducing ethinyl estradiol and progestin levels by 47% and 47% respectively
clinical_consequence: Contraceptive failure; unintended pregnancy
timing_notes: Use barrier or non-hormonal contraception; consider higher-dose OC only with counseling and close monitoring
high_risk_groups: all women of reproductive age with epilepsy

effect: Fluvoxamine inhibits CYP1A2, the primary enzyme for ropinirole metabolism, increasing ropinirole AUC by approximately 83%
clinical_consequence: Dopaminergic toxicity: nausea, dizziness, hypotension, hallucinations, somnolence; impulse control disorders
timing_notes: Reduce ropinirole dose when initiating fluvoxamine; monitor for adverse dopaminergic effects

effect: Magnesium-containing antacids worsen diarrhea induced by misoprostol due to combined osmotic and prostaglandin-mediated GI effects
clinical_consequence: Severe diarrhea, dehydration, electrolyte disturbances
timing_notes: Avoid magnesium-containing antacids during misoprostol therapy; use calcium-based antacids if needed

effect: Ketoconazole inhibits CYP3A4, increasing granisetron plasma concentrations; both prolong QT
clinical_consequence: QT prolongation and risk of arrhythmia
timing_notes: Monitor ECG during concurrent use; obtain baseline QTc; use alternative antifungal if possible

effect: Iron chelates methyldopa in the GI tract forming poorly absorbed complexes, reducing methyldopa bioavailability by up to 83%
clinical_consequence: Loss of blood pressure control; hypertensive episodes
timing_notes: Separate methyldopa and iron by at least 2 hours; monitor blood pressure

effect: Both vitamin D and atorvastatin are metabolized by CYP3A4; high-dose vitamin D may mildly compete for or modulate CYP3A4 activity
clinical_consequence: Minor changes in atorvastatin levels; generally not clinically significant at standard vitamin D doses
timing_notes: No action needed at vitamin D doses <4000 IU/day; monitor for statin side effects at very high vitamin D doses
high_risk_groups: patients on very high-dose vitamin D therapy (>10,000 IU/day)

effect: Psyllium fiber binds digoxin in the GI tract and reduces enterohepatic recirculation, decreasing digoxin bioavailability
clinical_consequence: Reduced digoxin levels; loss of rate control or heart failure management efficacy
timing_notes: Administer digoxin at least 2 hours before psyllium fiber; monitor digoxin levels
